seoTitle: How to Set Up a One-Click Mailto Button in Power BI
metaDescription: Set up a Power BI button that opens a pre-filled email using a mailto link, so viewers can share dashboard insights with one click instead of copying data.
intro: Sharing a quick update from a Power BI report usually means switching apps and typing everything from scratch. This tutorial shows how to build a button that triggers a mailto link, opening a ready-to-send email straight from the dashboard. It's a small feature that saves real time for anyone who regularly forwards reports or flags updates to a team.

steps.heading: Create the button in Power BI
steps.body: Insert a shape or button onto your report canvas and place it somewhere natural for users to reach after reviewing the data, such as near a summary section.

steps.heading: Set up the mailto action
steps.body: Enable the button's Action and set the type to Web URL, then enter a mailto: link as the address rather than a standard website URL.

steps.heading: Customize the email content
steps.body: Extend the mailto link with parameters for subject and body text so the email opens pre-filled with relevant context instead of a blank message.

steps.heading: Test the button's behavior
steps.body: Click the button in reading view to confirm it launches the default email client with the correct recipient, subject, and message pre-populated.

faq.question: Does the mailto button require an internet connection to work?
faq.answer: It relies on your device's default email application, so it needs that app configured, though the button itself works offline within Power BI Desktop.

faq.question: Can I include dynamic data like a person's name in the email subject?
faq.answer: Mailto links are typically static text strings, so dynamic personalization would require more advanced DAX-driven URL construction.

faq.question: Which email client opens when the button is clicked?
faq.answer: It opens whichever application is set as the default mail handler on the device, such as Outlook or the system's built-in mail app.

faq.question: Can I add multiple recipients to the mailto button?
faq.answer: Yes, mailto links support multiple addresses and even CC or BCC fields by adding the appropriate parameters to the link.
